name: Red-Eye Gravy
recipeIngredient: None (requires a good, well-cured country ham such as Smithfield or genuine Virginia ham)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry a slice of uncooked ham with most of the fat left on in its own fat until nicely browned on both sides.
2. Transfer the cooked ham to a warm platter and add boiling black coffee to the skillet, scraping the bottom and sides to dissolve any particles that cling to them.
3. Pour the resulting red-eye gravy over the ham and serve.
The name "red-eye gravy" (sometimes called "red-ham gravy") comes from the reddish liquid fat circle or oval that forms on the surface of the gravy when reduced, forming an "eye."
